llm.summary: Not ready for promotion. The shared enrichment-state contract, explicit `@ai-real` promotion rules, and the provided passing `npm run verify` result are in place, but exit criterion 1 is not satisfied in substance because the Mina credential boundary is not enforced as server-only. `src/features/ai/client.ts` reads `LLM_BASE`/`TOKEN`/`MODEL` and builds the bearer-auth request, yet unlike the repo's other server-side boundaries it is not marked `import "server-only"`; `src/features/enrichment/service.ts` is likewise not server-only. That leaves credential handling ambiguous by convention instead of enforced boundary, which the task's evaluator notes explicitly say should block promotion.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Criterion 2: notes and links now share one enrichment-state contract through Prisma fields plus shared enrichment types/helpers.
Criterion 3: the AI promotion contract is explicit in docs and queued task plans, including `@ai-real` and `npm run test:e2e -- --grep @ai-real` when AI env vars are configured.
Criterion 4: the supplied deterministic check shows `npm run verify` passed.
missing_requirements: Criterion 1 is not met in substance: the Mina AI integration path is not enforced as server-only, so token/base-url handling remains ambiguous.
Credential handling should be hardened so the AI client boundary cannot be imported into client-side code accidentally.
human_review_triggers: `src/features/ai/client.ts` handles secrets but is not protected with `server-only`, unlike the repository's other server-side boundaries.
`src/features/enrichment/service.ts` also lacks `server-only`, extending the same ambiguity around the intended server-only AI path.

summary: Not ready for promotion. The shared enrichment-state contract, explicit `@ai-real` promotion rules, and the provided passing `npm run verify` result are in place, but exit criterion 1 is not satisfied in substance because the Mina credential boundary is not enforced as server-only. `src/features/ai/client.ts` reads `LLM_BASE`/`TOKEN`/`MODEL` and builds the bearer-auth request, yet unlike the repo's other server-side boundaries it is not marked `import "server-only"`; `src/features/enrichment/service.ts` is likewise not server-only. That leaves credential handling ambiguous by convention instead of enforced boundary, which the task's evaluator notes explicitly say should block promotion.
